rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE contracts inside annex agree blocks with proper scope alignment.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE expressions reference visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Align data types in AGREE declarations with the actual AADL feature types.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Validate that assumption and guarantee expressions are well-formed and use valid AGREE syntax.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports or declared variables in valid scope.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the expected data types of the referenced features.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments in component type scopes.
Do not assume AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
Do not generate range assumptions without concrete bounds or visible feature references.
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner scope rules
